project.name: Raven
project.summary: ESP32 embedded firmware runtime providing a structured, event-driven execution model for autonomous vehicle/robotics applications. Built on top of ESP-IDF and FreeRTOS, it defines strict role boundaries between orchestration (Activities), hardware subsystem workers (Services), thread-safe shared state (Controllers), and an event-loop-backed broadcast bus (EventBus), all wired together at startup by a single root Coordinator.
project.core_principle: Every piece of responsibility has exactly one owner. Activities decide what the system does (mode/FSM); Services do the actual subsystem work and own hardware interaction; Controllers are the sole source of truth for shared data; EventBus carries broadcast facts without coupling producers to consumers; directed commands travel via typed task-queue messages. Business logic never executes inside an event callback — callbacks are thin translators that forward to the owning task's queue.

system_areas.name: Core Primitives
system_areas.purpose: Foundational base types shared across all runtime layers. Defines the message envelope and the reusable FreeRTOS task + queue wrapper that every Service and Activity builds on.

parts.role: BaseTask — C++ wrapper around a FreeRTOS task and its inbound queue. Provides start(), post_message(), and the handle_message() override point. All non-trivial work runs inside the owner task via this interface.
TaskMessage — minimal struct (kind, id, data*) that represents a directed command or self-message passed between components via their queues.

system_areas.name: Event Bus
system_areas.purpose: Decoupled broadcast layer for system-wide facts. Wraps the ESP-IDF default event loop so producers and consumers are never coupled. Handlers must stay thin — all logic executes in the receiving task.

parts.role: EventBus — static API (init, post, subscribe, unsubscribe) over esp_event_loop. The only place in the runtime where broadcast facts are published and received.
Defines NAVIGATION_EVENTS base and NAV_EVENT_MOVE_FORWARD_DONE — the reference example of a typed broadcast event published by a Service and consumed by an Activity.

system_areas.name: Shared State (Controllers)
system_areas.purpose: Thread-safe canonical state stores. The single source of truth for all system-wide data. Services write; Activities and other Services read. No tasks, no business logic — pure data with atomic or mutex protection.

parts.role: NavigationState — reference controller holding atomic x/y/z position floats. Illustrates the controller pattern: written exclusively by NavigationService, readable by any layer.
Aggregation header that exposes the full raven_state component surface.

system_areas.name: Services (Subsystem Workers)
system_areas.purpose: Asynchronous workers that encapsulate exactly one hardware subsystem or software stack. Each runs in its own FreeRTOS task, reads hardware / external input, writes results into shared state, and publishes broadcast events on noteworthy transitions. Receives directed commands via its queue.

parts.role: BaseService — thin specialization of BaseTask that marks a class as a subsystem worker. Concrete services inherit from this.
NavigationService — reference service. On MOVE_FORWARD command: increments NavigationState.x, then publishes NAV_EVENT_MOVE_FORWARD_DONE. Demonstrates the write-state → publish-event pattern.
nav_msg namespace — defines KIND (0x0010), MOVE_FORWARD, and MOVE_DONE message IDs used by both the service and the activity to identify directed messages.

system_areas.name: Activities (Orchestration / FSM)
system_areas.purpose: High-level behavioral layer. Each Activity owns a mode state machine and decides which services should be active and what they should do. Subscribes to events, receives directed messages, and posts commands to services — all processed in its own FreeRTOS task.

parts.role: BaseActivity — thin specialization of BaseTask that marks a class as an orchestration / FSM component.

NavigationActivity — reference activity with Idle/Working FSM. Demonstrates: subscribing to EventBus events via thin callback, delegating execution to NavigationService via directed message, and running all state transitions in its own task context.

system_areas.name: Bootstrap / Coordinator
system_areas.purpose: Root composition point. The only location where all objects are constructed, injected with their dependencies, and started. Contains no operational logic — it wires and launches.

parts.role: app_main — Coordinator entry point. Calls EventBus::init, constructs NavigationState, NavigationService (injected with state), NavigationActivity (injected with service), starts both tasks, then posts the initial directed commands to trigger the reference flow.
ESP-IDF project configuration (Wi-Fi SSID/password). Placeholder for build-time hardware configuration.

system_areas.name: Platform Abstraction
system_areas.purpose: Thin isolation layer between the runtime and ESP-IDF / hardware driver APIs. Keeps the rest of the codebase portable and driver-independent.

parts.role: Stub header marking the raven_platform component boundary. Intended to hold hardware init helpers and ESP-IDF driver wrappers as the project grows.

system_flows.name: Boot and initialization
system_flows.chain: app_main → EventBus::init (creates ESP-IDF event loop) → NavigationState constructed → NavigationService constructed (receives state ref) + started (spawns FreeRTOS task) → NavigationActivity constructed (receives service ref) + started (spawns FreeRTOS task, subscribes to NAVIGATION_EVENTS via EventBus)

system_flows.name: Directed command: MoveForward end-to-end
system_flows.chain: app_main posts TaskMessage{MOVE_FORWARD} to NavigationActivity queue → Activity dequeues in its task context → FSM check: Idle → Working → Activity posts TaskMessage{MOVE_FORWARD} to NavigationService queue → Service dequeues in its task context → increments NavigationState.x (atomic write) → publishes NAV_EVENT_MOVE_FORWARD_DONE via EventBus

system_flows.name: Event-driven FSM completion
system_flows.chain: EventBus delivers NAV_EVENT_MOVE_FORWARD_DONE → thin callback on_nav_event fires (runs on ESP-IDF event loop task) → callback posts TaskMessage{MOVE_DONE} to NavigationActivity queue and returns immediately → Activity dequeues MOVE_DONE in its own task context → FSM transitions Working → Idle

system_flows.name: Shared state read path
system_flows.chain: Any layer needing current position → reads NavigationState.x/y/z via atomic load (no mutex needed for scalar float reads) → observes latest value written by NavigationService

reading_order_for_new_sessions.reason: Defines the five layers (Activities, Services, Controllers, Coordinator, EventBus), their responsibilities, invariants, and the canonical interaction diagram. Read this first to load the mental model.
BaseTask and TaskMessage are the mechanical backbone of every Service and Activity. Understanding the task/queue/message pattern makes every other component immediately readable.
EventBus is the other half of the communication model (broadcast vs directed). Understand its thin API before reading any Service or Activity that publishes or subscribes.
NavigationState is the reference controller. It shows what a shared-state store looks like and why atomics (not mutexes) are used for simple scalars.
NavigationService is the reference Service implementation. Shows the write-state → publish-event pattern in concrete code.
NavigationActivity is the reference Activity. Shows FSM state management, thin EventBus callbacks, self-messaging, and directed command dispatch all in one place.
The Coordinator / bootstrap. Reading it last shows how all the pieces are wired and started — the system's composition root and the reference end-to-end flow trigger.

notes.role_of_this_file: project_map.json is a compact architectural orientation map intended to give a new agent or contributor a fast, high-signal mental model of the Raven runtime without requiring a full codebase read.
notes.what_this_file_is_not: This file is not an exact filesystem mirror, not a per-file inventory, not a changelog, not a task tracker, not a README rewrite, and not a full repository listing. It groups components by architectural meaning and includes only the most explanatory anchors for each system area.
notes.current_state: The repository contains a reference/scaffold implementation. NavigationService and NavigationActivity are working examples of each architectural pattern rather than production subsystems. raven_platform is a stub awaiting hardware driver abstractions.
